text:
Question:
What are the main voltage loss mechanisms in an SOFC? 

Options:
A. Activation polarization
B. Ohmic polarization
C. Concentration polarization
D. Internal currents and species crossover
question: What are the main voltage loss mechanisms in an SOFC?

text:
Question:
What are the advantages of SOFCs compared to other fuel cells? 

Options:
A. Fuel flexibility (hydrogen, methane, syngas)
B. High energy conversion efficiency
C. High-quality waste heat suitable for Combined Heat and Power (CHP)
D. Rapid startup at room temperature
question: What are the advantages of SOFCs compared to other fuel cells?

options.C: High-quality waste heat suitable for Combined Heat and Power (CHP)

text:
Question:
Which factors contribute to the performance degradation of Ni-YSZ anodes? 

Options:
A. Nickel particle sintering
B. Carbon deposition (Coking)
C. Sulfur poisoning
D. Instability caused by redox cycling
question: Which factors contribute to the performance degradation of Ni-YSZ anodes?

text:
Question:
The mechanisms involved in cathode "Chromium Poisoning" include: 

Options:
A. Generation of gaseous CrO2(OH)2 from interconnects
B. Migration of volatile Cr species to the cathode/electrolyte interface
C. Electrochemical reduction of gaseous Cr species to solid Cr2O3 at active sites
D. Reaction of Cr with surface-segregated Sr to form SrCrO4
question: The mechanisms involved in cathode "Chromium Poisoning" include:

options.A: Generation of gaseous CrO2(OH)2 from interconnects
options.B: Migration of volatile Cr species to the cathode/electrolyte interface
options.C: Electrochemical reduction of gaseous Cr species to solid Cr2O3 at active sites
options.D: Reaction of Cr with surface-segregated Sr to form SrCrO4

text:
Question:
Which parameters affect the ionic conductivity of YSZ? 

Options:
A. Temperature
B. Doping concentration (mol% Y)
C. Dopant cation radius
D. Grain size and grain boundary density
question: Which parameters affect the ionic conductivity of YSZ?

options.D: Grain size and grain boundary density

text:
Question:
What are the characteristics of LSCF cathode material?

Options:
A. High Mixed Ionic-Electronic Conductivity (MIEC)
B. High catalytic activity for oxygen reduction
C. Prone to Strontium (Sr) surface segregation
D. Excellent chemical compatibility with YSZ at temperatures above 1000°C
question: What are the characteristics of LSCF cathode material?

options.B: High catalytic activity for oxygen reduction
options.D: Excellent chemical compatibility with YSZ at temperatures above 1000°C

comments: D is false; it reacts to form insulating layers

text:
Question:
Advanced numerical models for evaluating SOFC performance typically couple which physical fields? 

Options:
A. Momentum transport (Navier-Stokes)
B. Species transport (Maxwell-Stefan)
C. Heat transfer
D. Charge transport (Ohm’s law / Butler-Volmer)
question: Advanced numerical models for evaluating SOFC performance typically couple which physical fields?

text:
Question:
Strategies to reduce coking on Ni-based anodes include: 

Options:
A. Increasing the Steam-to-Carbon (S/C) ratio
B. Adding Ceria (CeO2) or other oxygen storage materials
C. Replacing part of the Nickel with Copper (Cu) based alloys
D. Lowering the operating temperature to below 100°C
question: Strategies to reduce coking on Ni-based anodes include:

options.B: Adding Ceria (CeO2) or other oxygen storage materials
options.C: Replacing part of the Nickel with Copper (Cu) based alloys
options.D: Lowering the operating temperature to below 100°C

text:
Question:
Strategies to suppress Barium evaporation during the preparation of proton-conducting electrolytes (e.g., BZCYYb) include: 

Options:
A. Adding excess Barium during powder preparation
B. Lowering the sintering temperature
C. Adding sintering aids (e.g., NiO, ZnO, CuO)
D. Sintering in a reducing atmosphere
question: Strategies to suppress Barium evaporation during the preparation of proton-conducting electrolytes (e.g., BZCYYb) include:

options.A: Adding excess Barium during powder preparation
options.C: Adding sintering aids (e.g., NiO, ZnO, CuO)

text:
Question:
Which of the following materials can be used as electrolytes for SOFC or IT-SOFC? 

Options:
A. YSZ (Yttria-Stabilized Zirconia)
B. ScSZ (Scandia-Stabilized Zirconia)
C. GDC (Gadolinium-Doped Ceria)
D. LSCF (Lanthanum Strontium Cobalt Ferrite)
question: Which of the following materials can be used as electrolytes for SOFC or IT-SOFC?

text:
Question:
Voltage loss in the Activation Polarization region of the polarization curve mainly depends on: 

Options:
A. Exchange current density (i0)
B. Catalyst type and microstructure
C. Transfer coefficient ()
D. Electrolyte thickness
question: Voltage loss in the Activation Polarization region of the polarization curve mainly depends on:
comments: Thickness mainly affects the Ohmic region

text:
Question:
Which of the following descriptions regarding Internal Reforming in SOFCs are correct? 

Options:
A. Can occur directly on the anode nickel catalyst
B. Is an endothermic process, helping with stack thermal management
C. Requires an external high-pressure boiler
D. Allows direct use of natural gas as fuel
question: Which of the following descriptions regarding Internal Reforming in SOFCs are correct?

options.A: Can occur directly on the anode nickel catalyst
options.B: Is an endothermic process, helping with stack thermal management
options.D: Allows direct use of natural gas as fuel

text:
Question:
Which phenomena are directly related to "Concentration Polarization"? 

Options:
A. Hindered gas diffusion in porous electrodes
B. Reduced reactant partial pressure at active sites
C. Knudsen diffusion in small pores
D. Resistance of ions hopping between vacancies
question: Which phenomena are directly related to "Concentration Polarization"?

options.A: Hindered gas diffusion in porous electrodes
options.B: Reduced reactant partial pressure at active sites
options.D: Resistance of ions hopping between vacancies

text:
Question:
Contaminants that may cause chemical degradation of the cathode include: 

Options:
A. CO2 (forming carbonates)
B. SO2 (forming sulfates)
C. Chromium vapor (forming chromates)
D. Water vapor (promoting volatilization)
question: Contaminants that may cause chemical degradation of the cathode include:

text:
Question:
Key components of an SOFC "Balance of Plant" (BOP) typically include: 

Options:
A. Air blowers/compressors
B. Fuel preheaters and heat exchangers
C. DC-AC inverters
D. Dense ceramic electrolyte sheets
question: Key components of an SOFC "Balance of Plant" (BOP) typically include:
comments: Electrolyte is a cell component, not BOP

text:
Question:
What properties must an excellent SOFC electrolyte possess? 

Options:
A. High ionic conductivity at operating temperature
B. Almost no electronic conductivity
C. High mechanical strength and chemical stability
D. High gas permeability
question: What properties must an excellent SOFC electrolyte possess?

options.A: High ionic conductivity at operating temperature
options.C: High mechanical strength and chemical stability

text:
Question:
"Multi-field coupling" typically involves: 

Options:
A. Thermal stress caused by temperature gradients
B. Chemical stress (Chemical Expansion) caused by lattice non-stoichiometry
C. Mechanical damage such as delamination and cracking
D. Battery exterior color design
question: "Multi-field coupling" typically involves:

options.A: Thermal stress caused by temperature gradients
options.B: Chemical stress (Chemical Expansion) caused by lattice non-stoichiometry
options.C: Mechanical damage such as delamination and cracking

text:
Question:
Methods to increase the "Triple Phase Boundary (TPB)" length include: 

Options:
A. Reducing Ni and YSZ particle size in the cermet
B. Using composite electrodes instead of single-phase electrodes
C. Increasing sintering temperature leading to excessive grain growth
D. Using nanostructured or infiltrated catalysts
question: Methods to increase the "Triple Phase Boundary (TPB)" length include:

options.A: Reducing Ni and YSZ particle size in the cermet
options.B: Using composite electrodes instead of single-phase electrodes
options.C: Increasing sintering temperature leading to excessive grain growth

text:
Question:
Which descriptions regarding "Anode-Supported" SOFCs are correct?
 
Options:
A. Use a thin electrolyte to reduce ohmic resistance
B. Typically used for intermediate temperature operation
C. The anode serves as the mechanical support skeleton for the cell
D. Concentration polarization is typically lower than in electrolyte-supported cells
question: Which descriptions regarding "Anode-Supported" SOFCs are correct?

options.A: Use a thin electrolyte to reduce ohmic resistance
options.B: Typically used for intermediate temperature operation
options.C: The anode serves as the mechanical support skeleton for the cell
options.D: Concentration polarization is typically lower than in electrolyte-supported cells

text:
Question:
Sulfur poisoning causes Ni-YSZ anode performance decline because: 

Options:
A. Sulfur adsorbs onto active nickel surfaces
B. Blocks hydrogen adsorption and oxidation
C. Causes a sudden increase in activation polarization
D. Immediately causes YSZ lattice collapse
question: Sulfur poisoning causes Ni-YSZ anode performance decline because:

options.A: Sulfur adsorbs onto active nickel surfaces
options.C: Causes a sudden increase in activation polarization

text:
Question:
Which fuels are considered "fuel flexible" options for SOFCs? 

Options:
A. Natural Gas (Methane)
B. Syngas (H2/CO)
C. Methanol
D. Ammonia
question: Which fuels are considered "fuel flexible" options for SOFCs?

text:
Question:
Consequences of Sr segregation in LSCF include:
 
Options:
A. Loss of active surface area for ORR
B. Formation of insulating phases on the surface
C. Improved resistance to Chromium poisoning
D. Accelerated cathode performance degradation
question: Consequences of Sr segregation in LSCF include:

options.A: Loss of active surface area for ORR
options.B: Formation of insulating phases on the surface

text:
Question:
In the Nernst equation, which variables depend on the operating temperature? 

Options:
A. Standard electrode potential (E0)
B. Gas constant (R)
C. The actual temperature (T) in the RT/nF term
D. Gas activity/partial pressure (if in a closed system or equilibrium affected by T)
question: In the Nernst equation, which variables depend on the operating temperature?

options.C: The actual temperature (T) in the RT/nF term
options.D: Gas activity/partial pressure (if in a closed system or equilibrium affected by T)

text:
Question:
Analytical techniques commonly used to study SOFC degradation include: 

Options:
A. Electrochemical Impedance Spectroscopy (EIS)
B. Scanning Electron Microscopy (SEM)
C. X-ray Photoelectron Spectroscopy (XPS)
D. 3D Microstructure Reconstruction (FIB-SEM/X-ray CT)
question: Analytical techniques commonly used to study SOFC degradation include:

text:
Question:
Common SOFC stack structural configurations include: 

Options:
A. Planar
B. Tubular
C. Hollow-fiber
D. Flat-tube (Segmented-in-series)
question: Common SOFC stack structural configurations include:

text:
Question:
Why are SOFCs considered an environmentally friendly technology? 

Options:
A. Extremely low emissions of NOx, SOx, and particulate matter
B. Significantly reduced CO2 emissions compared to thermal power plants (due to high efficiency)
C. Low water consumption
D. No byproducts whatsoever
question: Why are SOFCs considered an environmentally friendly technology?

options.A: Extremely low emissions of NOx, SOx, and particulate matter
options.B: Significantly reduced CO2 emissions compared to thermal power plants (due to high efficiency)

comments: C is usually true; D is false as water/CO2 are byproducts

text:
Question:
Challenges facing large-scale commercialization of SOFCs include: 

Options:
A. High initial capital cost
B. Long-term durability over 40,000 hours
C. Thermal cycling stability during system startup/shutdown
D. Lack of fuel flexibility
question: Challenges facing large-scale commercialization of SOFCs include:

options.C: Thermal cycling stability during system startup/shutdown

text:
Question:
What occurs at the "Exchange Current Density" state on a microscopic level?
 
Options:
A. Forward reaction rate equals reverse reaction rate
B. Net current is zero
C. The system is in electrochemical equilibrium
D. Voltage loss is at its maximum
question: What occurs at the "Exchange Current Density" state on a microscopic level?

options.A: Forward reaction rate equals reverse reaction rate
options.C: The system is in electrochemical equilibrium
options.D: Voltage loss is at its maximum

text:
Question:
Factors affecting the "Ohmic Resistance" of an SOFC include: 

Options:
A. Electrolyte thickness
B. Electrolyte ionic conductivity
C. Contact resistance between components
D. Electronic conductivity of interconnects and current collectors
question: Factors affecting the "Ohmic Resistance" of an SOFC include:

options.D: Electronic conductivity of interconnects and current collectors

text:
Question:
Advanced numerical simulation methods for SOFC mechanical damage include: 

Options:
A. Linear Elastic Fracture Mechanics (LEFM)
B. Cohesive Zone Model (CZM)
C. Phase Field Method (PFM)
D. Peridynamics
question: Advanced numerical simulation methods for SOFC mechanical damage include:
